With today's Issue the woman's
suffrage discussion closes. But the
ballots will run until Wednesday,
20 that all who desire to may vote.
Unusual interest In the subject has
been displayed by many Of the
local club women Of the city, both
by those who advocate the cause
and those who oppose the enfran.
chisement. A number Of interest.
ing articles expressing both views
Of the question have been literally
contributed to The Press, and there
are still others.

fragette," said Mrs. J. e. Candy,
Il believe the privilege of voting
belongs to every citizen. I think
that women are as qualified to be
citizens and have equal rights. and
more 30. than some men. especially
foreigners. They are superior in
intelligence to some of the ignor-
ant foreigners who, after they be-
come naturalized, are given a voice
In government affairs.


Women are expected to abide
by the laws of the country, there
fore 1t 10 only just that they should
have an equal right in making
them. !. appreciate all laws made
for the protection of women, but
